title: params should be preferred instead of varargs
description: "params" should be used instead of "varargs"Analyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?A method using theVarArgscalling convention is not Common Language Specification (CLS) compliant and might not be accessible across programming languages, while theparamskeyword works the same way and is CLS compliant.This rule raises an issue when apublicorprotectedtype contains apublicorprotectedmethod that uses theVarArgscalling convention.Noncompliant code exampleusing System; namespace MyLibrary { public class Foo { public void Bar(__arglist) // Noncompliant { ArgIterator argumentIterator = new ArgIterator(__arglist); for(int i = 0; i < argumentIterator.GetRemainingCount(); i++) { Console.WriteLine( __refvalue(argumentIterator.GetNextArg(), string)); } } }
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects public or protected methods that use __arglist varargs calling convention
logic.triggering_conditions: Method is public or protected AND uses __arglist parameter
logic.exclusion_conditions: Test methods, methods with Assert/Console/Logger/Debug calls, generated code
logic.reasoning: __arglist varargs calling convention is not CLS compliant and might not be accessible across programming languages, while params keyword is CLS compliant and provides the same functionality

should_trigger: public void Bar(__arglist) { ArgIterator argumentIterator = new ArgIterator(__arglist); }
should_not_trigger: public void Bar(params string[] args) { foreach (string arg in args) { Console.WriteLine(arg); } }
